<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11418515]] Wikidata name: Tōjin Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Higashikushira, Kagoshima|Higashikushira, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Higashikushira, Kagoshima|Higashikushira]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of [[:en:Ōsumi Peninsula|Ōsumi Peninsula]] in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|Kimotsuki]]) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Kanoya, Kagoshima|Kanoya]]) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ([[:en:Ōsaki, Kagoshima|Ōsaki]]) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]], the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from [[:en:Shibushi Station|Shibushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nichinan Line|Nichinan Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11418515]] Wikidata name: Tōjin Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of {{ill|Ōsumi Peninsula|en|Ōsumi Peninsula|WD=Q386078|lt=Ōsumi Peninsula}} in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|en|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|WD=Q1350770|lt=Kimotsuki}}) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|WD=Q819578|lt=Kanoya}}) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ({{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt=Ōsaki}}) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tojin Kofun Group}} *[https://www.welcomekyushu.jp/project/yamashiro-tumulus/tumulus/33 Welcome Kyushu Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tojin Kofun Group]][[en:Tōjin Kofun Cluster]][[ja:唐仁古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11418515]] Wikidata name: Tōjin Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of {{ill|Ōsumi Peninsula|en|Ōsumi Peninsula|WD=Q386078|lt=Ōsumi Peninsula}} in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|en|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|WD=Q1350770|lt=Kimotsuki}}) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|WD=Q819578|lt=Kanoya}}) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ({{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt=Ōsaki}}) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}, the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tojin Kofun Group}} *[https://www.welcomekyushu.jp/project/yamashiro-tumulus/tumulus/33 Welcome Kyushu Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira, Kagoshima|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of {{ill|Ōsumi Peninsula|en|Ōsumi Peninsula|WD=Q386078|lt=Ōsumi Peninsula|1=Ōsumi Peninsula}} in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|en|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|WD=Q1350770|lt=Kimotsuki|1=Kimotsuki, Kagoshima}}) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|WD=Q819578|lt=Kanoya|1=Kanoya, Kagoshima}}) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ({{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tojin Kofun Group}} *[https://www.welcomekyushu.jp/project/yamashiro-tumulus/tumulus/33 Welcome Kyushu Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira, Kagoshima|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of {{ill|Ōsumi Peninsula|en|Ōsumi Peninsula|WD=Q386078|lt=Ōsumi Peninsula|1=Ōsumi Peninsula}} in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|en|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|WD=Q1350770|lt=Kimotsuki|1=Kimotsuki, Kagoshima}}) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|WD=Q819578|lt=Kanoya|1=Kanoya, Kagoshima}}) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ({{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tojin Kofun Group}} *[https://www.welcomekyushu.jp/project/yamashiro-tumulus/tumulus/33 Welcome Kyushu Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Infobox ancient site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | native_name =唐仁古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg; File:Tojin Otsuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Tōjin Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan#Japan Kagoshima Prefecture | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tōjin Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|31.364667|N|130.986778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira, Kagoshima|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Higashikushira|WD=Q1206619|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|21|11.40|N|130|58|2.96|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tōjin Kofun Cluster'''|唐仁古墳群}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofu|Kofuns]] located in the Shinkawanishi neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|en|Higashikushira, Kagoshima|WD=Q1206619|lt=Higashikushira|1=Higashikushira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site been protected as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1934.<ref>{{cite web|URL=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2986|title=唐仁古墳群|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|access-date=16 October 2021}}</ref> It is the largest group of Kofuns in Kagoshima Prefecture, and includes the largest ''[[kofun]]'' in the prefecture, the {{nihongo|'''Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun'''|唐仁大塚古墳}}
== Overview== The Tōjin Kofun Cluster is built in a sand dune belt on a slightly elevated area at an elevation of five to seven meters, on the left bank of the downstream of the Kimosuke River, on the coast of Shibushi Bay on the east coast of {{ill|Ōsumi Peninsula|en|Ōsumi Peninsula|WD=Q386078|lt=Ōsumi Peninsula|1=Ōsumi Peninsula}} in eastern Kagoshima Prefecture. In the surrounding area are the [[Tsukazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|en|Kimotsuki, Kagoshima|WD=Q1350770|lt=Kimotsuki|1=Kimotsuki, Kagoshima}}) to the south, the [[Okazaki Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Kanoya, Kagoshima|en|Kanoya, Kagoshima|WD=Q819578|lt=Kanoya|1=Kanoya, Kagoshima}}) to the west, and the Yokose/Jinryo Kofun Cluster ({{ill|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|en|Ōsaki, Kagoshima|WD=Q931439|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Kagoshima}}) to the north. Of these, the Tōjin Kofun Cluster consists of a total of 130 Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''[[Zenpōkōenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, 119 {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} round tumuli, and eight tumuli whose structure is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun No.1 (Tōjin Ōtsuka Kofun) measures 140 meters in length, (185 meters including surrounding moat) and height of 11 meters, making it the largest tumulus in Kagoshima Prefecture, and third largest in Kyushu. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}, the Ōtsuka Jinja, is located on top of the tumulus, and five lid stones of the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are exposed below the shrine buildings. The burial chamber contained a sandstone boat-shaped sarcophagus with a length of 2.75 meters and a width of 0.87 meters, and remains of iron armor were found between the north wall and the sarcophagus. It is estimated that this tumulus was built in the late 4th century, in the latter half of the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Most of the ''kofun'' are small-sized round tumuli, with the exception of the three ''zenpō-kōen-fun'' (Kofun No. 1, 16, and 100) and the medium-sized round tumuli (Kofun No. 17 and 33). Furthermore, no underground horizontal cave tombs have been confirmed, in which the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is dug horizontally from the bottom of a vertical pit have been found in the area, although this style was popular in the Ōsumi Peninsula around the middle of the Kofun period. This could be because the area was geologically unsuitable, being on a sand dune area, or else it could be an indication that the local clan had a close social and political relationship with the [[:en:Yamato Kingdom|Yamato Kingdom]] in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Yakushidozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.16(Yakushidozuka Kofun) Tojin Kofun Group-33, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.33(Mukaizuka Kofun) Yakushozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.100(Yakushozuka Kofun) </gallery>
The site is approximately 35 minutes by car from {{ill|Shibushi Station|en|Shibushi Station|WD=Q7496088|lt=Shibushi Station|1=Shibushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|WD=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|WD=Q6573269|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagoshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline|Tojin Kofun Group}} *[https://www.welcomekyushu.jp/project/yamashiro-tumulus/tumulus/33 Welcome Kyushu Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
